message:
test(lifecycle): stale-refresh config contract in state.index (ibuuh.24)

ibuuh.24 stale-refresh row. The world-class stale-refresh architecture
requires agents to read stale_threshold_seconds from cass health so they
can reason about when a refresh is warranted, imminent, or overdue. A
drift that drops or renames that field forces agents to guess the
threshold — over-refreshing hits machine load, under-refreshing leaves
stale data.

New test index_readiness_exposes_stale_refresh_config asserts:
 * state.index.stale_threshold_seconds is a positive integer in the
   sane bound [60s, 86400s]; catches flipped-sign, unit-confusion
   (ms vs s), and default-drift regressions
 * state.index.{fresh, stale, exists, rebuilding} are all bool-typed
 * state.index.status is one of the documented enum values
   (missing | fresh | stale | rebuilding | unknown)

VERIFICATION:
cargo test --test lifecycle_matrix index_readiness: 1/1 pass.

Follow-up slice on coding_agent_session_search-ibuuh.24; bead stays
open for the full world-class refresh architecture.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
